function.name: file_system_read
function.description: Reads the contents of a file as text. Supports three modes: (1) read the whole file, (2) specify a line range with start_line/end_line (1-indexed), or (3) specify exact line numbers with the lines parameter. The lines parameter is designed to be used with grep results — pass specific line numbers directly. Results are capped at approximately 50k tokens — if the file is larger, content is truncated with a notice.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file.

start_line.description: Starting line number (1-indexed, inclusive). Use with end_line for a range.

end_line.description: Ending line number (1-indexed, inclusive). Use with start_line for a range.

lines.description: Specific 1-indexed line numbers to read (e.g. [42, 45, 47]). Use with grep results to read only matching lines instead of large ranges. Each line is prefixed with its number for easy reference.

function.name: file_system_list
function.description: Lists the contents of a directory — files and subdirectories with name, type, size, and last modified time.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the directory (defaults to home_dir).

function.name: file_system_search
function.description: Searches for files matching a glob pattern (e.g. **/*.py, **/*.md). Returns matching paths with file sizes.

root.description: Root directory to search from (defaults to home_dir).

function.name: file_system_info
function.description: Returns metadata about a file or directory — size, last modified time, type (file/dir/symlink), and permissions.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file or directory.

function.name: file_system_write
function.description: Creates a new file or overwrites an existing file with the given content. Permission-gated — the user will be prompted to approve. Restricted to the home_dir (~/Documents) — path escape attempts are blocked.

path.description: Absolute or relative path (must resolve inside home_dir).

content.description: The content to write to the file.

function.name: file_system_append
function.description: Appends content to the end of an existing file. Creates the file if it does not exist. Permission-gated — the user will be prompted to approve. Restricted to the home_dir (~/Documents) — path escape attempts are blocked.

path.description: Absolute or relative path (must resolve inside home_dir).

content.description: The content to append to the file.

function.name: file_system_patch
function.description: Replaces the first occurrence of old_text with new_text in a file. Uses exact literal text matching (not regex). Permission-gated — the user will be prompted to approve. Restricted to the home_dir (~/Documents) — path escape attempts are blocked.

path.description: Absolute or relative path (must resolve inside home_dir).

old_text.description: Exact text to replace (first occurrence only, literal match).

function.name: file_system_create
function.description: Creates a new file at the given path. Errors if the file already exists (use file_system_write to overwrite). Parent directories are created automatically. NOT permission-gated — no approval required. Restricted to the home_dir (~/Documents) — path escape attempts are blocked.

path.description: Absolute or relative path for the new file (must resolve inside home_dir).

content.description: Optional initial content for the file (default empty).

function.name: file_system_trash
function.description: Permanently deletes a file or empty directory. Permission-gated — the user will be prompted to approve. Restricted to the home_dir (~/Documents) — path escape attempts are blocked.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file or empty directory to delete (must resolve inside home_dir).
